query_normalized: the impact of social comparison on self-perception in ai-generated image platforms psychology
rationale: The paper focuses on natural language processing techniques (POS tagging) for code-mixed text on social media, which is a distinct technical construct from the psychological mechanisms of social comparison and self-perception. Although both mention "social media," the paper lacks any measurable connection to the user's variables (social comparison, self-perception) or the specific domain of AI-generated image platforms, falling under the rejection rule for distinct constructs sharing only homony
